Problem Statement: In the following exercises, ⓐ graph the quadratic functions on the same rectangular coordinate system and ⓑ describe what effect adding a constant, k , to the function has on the basic parabola.
Question: $$f ( x ) = x^{2} , g ( x ) = x^{2} + 4 ,$$ and $$h ( x ) = x^{2} - 4 .$$
Answer: ⓐ ⓑ The graph of $$g ( x ) = x^{2} + 4$$ is the same as the graph of $$f ( x ) = x^{2}$$ but shifted up 4 units. The graph of $$h ( x ) = x^{2} - 4$$ is the same as the graph of $$f ( x ) = x^{2}$$ but shift down 4 units.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, ⓐ graph the quadratic functions on the same rectangular coordinate system and ⓑ describe what effect adding a constant, $$h$$ , inside the parentheses has
Question: $$f ( x ) = x^{2} , g ( x ) = ( x - 3 )^{2} ,$$ and $$h ( x ) = ( x + 3 )^{2} .$$
Answer: ⓐ ⓑ The graph of $$g ( x ) = ( x - 3 )^{2}$$ is the same as the graph of $$f ( x ) = x^{2}$$ but shifted right 3 units. The graph of $$h ( x ) = ( x + 3 )^{2}$$ is the same as the graph of $$f ( x ) = x^{2}$$ but shifted left 3 units.
